key_insight: A two-stage conversion-and-reinforcement pipeline can make sliding-window attention nearly as effective as full self-attention for math reasoning, while keeping linear-complexity inference.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical way to retrofit existing long-context LLMs for cheaper, more scalable inference without training a new base model from scratch.
method: Convert a pretrained self-attention model to sliding-window attention with supervised fine-tuning.
Apply on-policy reinforcement learning so the model adapts its trajectories to the sliding-window constraint.
Use the RL stage to reduce the data-architecture mismatch that hurts SWA after SFT.
evidence_signal: On mathematical reasoning benchmarks, the method substantially narrows the accuracy gap between sliding-window attention and self-attention, recovering much of the loss after conversion.
watch_item: The approach depends on the quality and stability of RL adaptation, and performance may still be sensitive to tasks with strong long-range dependencies beyond the window.
paper_title: Architecture-Aware Reinforcement Learning Makes Sliding-Window Attention Competitive in Math Reasoning
